questions.question: What does BEM stand for?
questions.explanation: BEM stands for Block Element Modifier, a CSS naming methodology.

questions.question: Which is the correct BEM syntax for an element?
questions.explanation: Elements use double underscore: .block__element.

questions.question: Which is the correct BEM syntax for a modifier?
questions.explanation: Modifiers use double dash: .block--modifier.

questions.question: What is wrong with .block__element__subelement?
options: BEM discourages nesting elements — use a new block instead
questions.explanation: BEM does not use element-of-element. If a sub-element is needed, consider making it a separate block.

questions.question: When should you use a BEM modifier?
options: For variants or state changes of a block or element
questions.explanation: Modifiers represent variants (size, theme) or states (active, disabled) of blocks or elements.

questions.question: What is the block in BEM?
options: A standalone component that has meaning on its own
questions.explanation: A block is a semantically independent component that can be reused across the project.

questions.question: What is an element in BEM?
options: A part of a block that has no standalone meaning
questions.explanation: An element is a part of a block that makes sense only within its block context.

questions.question: How do you handle a button inside a card in BEM?
questions.explanation: Buttons are their own block (.btn). BEM encourages composing blocks rather than nesting elements.

questions.question: What specificity do BEM selectors have?
questions.explanation: BEM uses only class selectors, keeping all specificity at the same level (0,0,1,0).

questions.question: Why does BEM avoid element selectors?
options: They create specificity and break if HTML structure changes
questions.explanation: Element selectors couple CSS to specific HTML tags, increasing specificity and fragility.

questions.question: Which naming is correct for a 'large' modifier on a title element?
questions.explanation: Element modifier: .block__element--modifier (.card__title--large).

questions.question: What is a problem with too many BEM modifiers?
options: They create combinatorial explosion and HTML bloat
questions.explanation: Too many modifier combinations create excessive CSS and HTML class lists. Consider a new block.

questions.question: Can BEM blocks contain other BEM blocks?
questions.explanation: BEM blocks can contain other blocks (composition). A .card can contain a .btn block.

questions.question: What does BEM stand for in terms of naming philosophy?
options: Block = component, Element = part, Modifier = variant
questions.explanation: Block = independent component, Element = part of block, Modifier = variant/state.

questions.question: How do you name a modifier that applies only to an element?
questions.explanation: Element modifiers use: .block__element--modifier.

questions.question: What should you do when a component has too many modifier variants?
questions.explanation: When a component has too many variant combinations, it may be time to split it into multiple distinct blocks.

questions.question: Does BEM work with CSS preprocessors like Sass?
options: Yes — Sass &.element and &--modifier syntax works well with BEM
questions.explanation: Sass's & selector works perfectly with BEM for nesting block, element, and modifier classes.

questions.question: What is the difference between BEM and utility-first CSS?
options: BEM is component-oriented; utility-first uses atomic classes
questions.explanation: BEM groups styles by component; utility-first uses single-purpose classes composed in HTML.

questions.question: How do you name SVG icons in BEM?
questions.explanation: Icons are typically their own block (.icon) as they have standalone meaning.

questions.question: What is the BEM approach to responsive design?
options: Use BEM for base classes + media queries for overrides
questions.explanation: BEM handles component structure; media queries adjust component styles at breakpoints using the same BEM classes.
